commands.review_summary:
The PR teaches memory-core to adapt explicit generic `embeddingProviders` into memory embedding providers, updates focused tests, and revises plugin docs plus changelog text.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report. Source review confirms current main lacks the generic-provider memory bridge, and the PR tests cover the intended new behavior with mocked registries.
commands.review_followup: Contributor proof plus maintainer API/boundary approval is needed; there is no narrow automated code repair indicated by this review.

target.title: feat(memory-core): consume generic embedding providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Teach memory-core to resolve explicit embedding configs from the generic `embeddingProviders` contract added in #84947.
- Preserve the existing memory-specific adapter path and `auto` selection behavior, so generic providers are opt-in for memory until configured explicitly.
- Document the relationship between generic embedding providers and the memory-core adapter surface.

Stacked on #84947 via `mbelinky/84947-general-embedding-providers-base`.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: memory-core can consume a generic embedding provider by explicit provider id without changing memory auto-selection or overriding memory-specific providers.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout plus AWS Crabbox focused remote run.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/embedding-provider.contract.test.ts src/plugins/embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-embedding-providers.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-embedding-providers.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-embedding-providers.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-embedding-providers.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugin-sdk/embedding-providers.ts src/plugins/contracts/embedding-provider.contract.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts src/plugins/types.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`; `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/manifest.md docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md docs/plugins/adding-capabilities.md`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: local focused checks passed; AWS Crabbox focused run `run_b79d455d8c85` passed the same relevant unit, type, SDK API, docs, and diff checks after manual no-hydrate setup.
Observed result after fix: legacy memory providers remain authoritative for the same id, `auto` stays memory-only, and explicit generic provider configs are adapted into memory-core.
What was not tested: no live third-party embedding endpoint was called in this PR; that belongs to the follow-up OpenAI-compatible generic provider PR.
target.author: mbelinky
